fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to build out the logging config engine we've been talking about. Basically operators are really frustrated that every time we deploy to a new environment they have to go into the code and manually change logging stuff — it's causing drift between staging and prod and we've had a couple of incidents because of it. The idea is to have an XML-based config system where the logging behavior is driven by the environment at runtime, not hardcoded. Similar to how we handled the conditional logic in that Spring profile integration we did before — you know the one. We also need a way to pull in shared config fragments so we're not copy-pasting the same blocks everywhere.

On top of that, there's a typed settings binding layer — operators need to be able to set things like whether logging is on/off, what port strategy to use, filter includes/excludes etc. via flat config keys and have those map to a proper structured object with sensible defaults.

The whole thing needs a test harness that reads JSON test cases and writes outputs to files for comparison. Output errors should be friendly normalized categories, never raw exceptions. Needs to be modular — don't just dump everything in one file. Let me know if you have questions, I'll try to dig up the old design notes.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: configure(entry: str, documents: dict[str, str], properties: dict[str, str], profiles: list[str]) -> ConfigureResult; bind(properties: dict[str, str]) -> BindResult

trigger_keywords: when should a variable declaration be rejected
what makes a property declaration malformed
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A springProperty declaration is rejected (and tallied as one error) when its 'name' attribute is empty/omitted OR its 'source' attribute is empty/omitted. Both conditions independently trigger rejection. Valid declarations with a missing environment key simply resolve to empty string (or their fallback). The error count in output reflects only structurally invalid declarations, not missing environment keys.

trigger_keywords: how are variables referenced in pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Resolved variables are interpolated using the ${name} syntax inside pattern strings. For example, a pattern containing ${console_pattern_prefix}${console_pattern_body}${console_pattern_suffix} will have each placeholder replaced with the resolved value of the corresponding named variable. XML entity references like " in pattern text must be decoded to their character equivalents (e.g., " becomes ") before output.

trigger_keywords: what happens with blank profile expression
profile block with no name attribute
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A springProfile block with an empty or blank name expression is NEVER kept — it and all its contents are dropped unconditionally, regardless of active profiles. This is distinct from negated profiles: a block like <springProfile name="!"> is also dropped. Only non-empty name expressions (positive or negated with !) are evaluated against active profiles.

A profile expression prefixed with '!' is negated: the block is kept when the named profile (the part after '!') is NOT in the active profiles set. For example, name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-console" is kept only when 'logback-access-test-disable-default-console' is absent from active profiles. The '!' must be the leading character of the expression.

trigger_keywords: inner springProfile when outer is dropped
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Profile blocks can nest. An inner springProfile block is evaluated ONLY if its enclosing outer block was kept. If the outer block is dropped (its expression did not match), all descendant blocks are dropped unconditionally regardless of their own expressions. This cascading drop behavior applies to all levels of nesting.

The <include resource="filename"/> element causes the engine to look up the named document from the supplied document set by the exact string value of the 'resource' attribute. The referenced document's root element should be <included>. All declarations inside the included document (springProperty, appender, appender-ref, springProfile) participate fully in resolution as if they were inline. If the resource name is not found in the document set, it should be treated as a recoverable error.

trigger_keywords: ordering of appender lines in output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Appenders are reported in the order their <appender-ref> elements are encountered during document processing (declaration order). For included documents, the include is processed inline at the point where it appears. The output is one 'appender=<name> pattern=<resolved-pattern>' line per active appender-ref, followed by a single 'errors=<count>' line.

trigger_keywords: what are the defaults for bind mode
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The typed settings defaults are: enabled=true, config=<null> (absent), local_port_strategy=server, tomcat.request_attributes_enabled=<null> (absent), undertow.record_request_start_time=true, tee_filter.enabled=false, tee_filter.includes=<null> (absent), tee_filter.excludes=<null> (absent). Absent optional fields render as '<null>'. The enumerated local_port_strategy is rendered lowercase.

trigger_keywords: what prefix do bind properties use
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: All bind-mode property keys use the prefix 'logback.access.' followed by the field path with dots and hyphens (e.g., 'logback.access.enabled', 'logback.access.tee-filter.includes', 'logback.access.tomcat.request-attributes-enabled'). Hyphens in the key correspond to underscores in the output field name (kebab-case input, snake_case output rendering).

Failures in the execution adapter must be rendered as normalized 'error=<category>' lines. Valid categories include: invalid_request, entry_not_found, config_failed, bind_failed, unknown_mode, empty_input. Raw exception class names or stack traces must never appear in stdout. Each error scenario maps to exactly one category line.

trigger_keywords: where are test output files written
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The test harness writes one file per case to rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt (e.g., feature1_spring_property_resolution@000.txt). The <cases-dir> namespace prevents different case directories from overwriting each other. Files contain only raw stdout from the program under test — no PASS/FAIL metadata. The default cases dir is 'public_test_cases' and can be overridden with --cases-dir.

trigger_keywords: variable fallback when key absent from environment
what value when source key not in properties
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When a springProperty's source key is absent from the supplied properties map, the 'defaultValue' attribute is used as the resolved value. If neither the source key is present nor a defaultValue is specified, the variable resolves to an empty string (not an error — this is valid). Only structurally malformed declarations (empty/missing name or source) are counted as errors.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Here are the public test cases for feature1_spring_property_resolution:
{
  "description": "A configuration document declares named variables that are each sourced from a key in a supplied property environment, with an optional literal fallback used when the key is absent from the environment. A declaration whose name or source key is empty or omitted is rejected and tallied. The resolved variables are interpolated into a console output pattern; the engine reports every active output target with its fully resolved pattern, followed by the number of rejected declarations.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "mode": "configure",
        "entry": "main.xml",
        "documents": {
          "main.xml": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_prefix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.prefix"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_body"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.body"
            defaultValue="%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_suffix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.suffix"
            defaultValue=";"/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_blank" source=""/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_missing"/>
    <springProperty name="" source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-blank"/>
    <springProperty source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-missing"/>
    <appender name="console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
        <encoder>
            <pattern>${console_pattern_prefix}${console_pattern_body}${console_pattern_suffix}</pattern>
        </encoder>
    </appender>
    <appender-ref ref="console"/>
</configuration>
"
        },
        "properties": {
          "logback.access.test.console.pattern.prefix": ">>>",
          "logback.access.test.console.pattern.suffix": "<<<"
        }
      },
      "expected_output": "appender=console pattern=>>>%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b<<<
errors=4"
    },
    {
      "input": {
        "mode": "configure",
        "entry": "main.xml",
        "documents": {
          "main.xml": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_prefix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.prefix"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_body"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.body"
            defaultValue="%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_suffix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.suffix"
            defaultValue=";"/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_blank" source=""/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_missing"/>
    <springProperty name="" source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-blank"/>
    <springProperty source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-missing"/>
    <appender name="console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
        <encoder>
            <pattern>${console_pattern_prefix}${console_pattern_body}${console_pattern_suffix}</pattern>
        </encoder>
    </appender>
    <appender-ref ref="console"/>
</configuration>
"
        },
        "properties": {}
      },
      "expected_output": "appender=console pattern=%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b;
errors=4"
    }
  ]
}

hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Here are the public test cases for feature3_spring_profile_selection:
{
  "description": "A configuration document gates sections behind profile expressions. A section is kept only when its expression matches the set of active profiles; a leading negation marker inverts the match, sections may nest (an inner section is evaluated only if its enclosing section is kept), and an empty expression is never kept. The engine reports, in declaration order, the output targets belonging to the kept sections together with their patterns, followed by an error count.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "mode": "configure",
        "entry": "main.xml",
        "documents": {
          "main.xml": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-console">
        <appender name="default_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>default_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="default_console"/>
        <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-nested-console">
            <appender name="default_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>default_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="default_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-console">
        <appender name="additional_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>additional_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="additional_console"/>
        <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-nested-console">
            <appender name="additional_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>additional_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="additional_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-empty"/>
    <springProfile name="">
        <appender name="ignored_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>ignored_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="ignored_console"/>
    </springProfile>
</configuration>
"
        },
        "profiles": [
          "logback-access-test-disable-default-console",
          "logback-access-test-enable-additional-console",
          "logback-access-test-enable-additional-nested-console"

appender=additional_nested_console pattern=additional_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b
errors=0"
    },
    {
      "input": {
        "mode": "configure",
        "entry": "main.xml",
        "documents": {
          "main.xml": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-console">
        <appender name="default_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>

feature3_spring_profile_selection.json.description: A configuration document gates sections behind profile expressions. A section is kept only when its expression matches the set of active profiles; a leading negation marker inverts the match, sections may nest (an inner section is evaluated only if its enclosing section is kept), and an empty expression is never kept. The engine reports, in declaration order, the output targets belonging to the kept sections together with their patterns, followed by an error count.

        <appender-ref ref="additional_console"/>
        <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-nested-console">
            <appender name="additional_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>additional_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="additional_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-empty"/>
    <springProfile name="">
        <appender name="ignored_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>ignored_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="ignored_console"/>
    </springProfile>
</configuration>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-console">
        <appender name="default_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">

        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="default_console"/>
        <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-nested-console">
            <appender name="default_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>default_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="default_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-console">
        <appender name="additional_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>additional_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="additional_console"/>
        <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-nested-console">
            <appender name="additional_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>additional_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="additional_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-empty"/>
    <springProfile name="">
        <appender name="ignored_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>ignored_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="ignored_console"/>
    </springProfile>
</configuration>

feature4_configuration_property_binding..description: Binds a flat map of dotted configuration keys under a fixed prefix onto a typed, immutable settings object with nested groups. Absent keys take documented defaults; supplied keys override them, including an enumerated strategy value and nested boolean/string groups. The effective value of every field is reported in a stable order, with absent optional fields rendered as a neutral null marker.

cases.expected_output:
enabled=true
config=<null>
local_port_strategy=server
tomcat.request_attributes_enabled=<null>
undertow.record_request_start_time=true
tee_filter.enabled=false
tee_filter.includes=<null>
tee_filter.excludes=<null>

enabled=false
config=classpath:logback-access-test.xml
local_port_strategy=server
tomcat.request_attributes_enabled=true
undertow.record_request_start_time=false
tee_filter.enabled=false
tee_filter.includes=example.com
tee_filter.excludes=localhost
